title: Hieracium prolixiceps
taxonomy: Hieracium prolixiceps is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it in the order Asterales, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics. At the genus level, Hieracium prolixiceps is part of the large and diverse genus Hieracium, consisting of over 300 species. Within the genus, Hieracium prolixiceps is further classified as a species of hawkweed, a group of plants known for their yellow or white flowers. The specific classification of Hieracium prolixiceps helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships and place it within the broader context of plant taxonomy.

intro: Hieracium prolixiceps is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a hawkweed. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium prolixiceps include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium prolixiceps is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or ointment, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including skin irritations, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
